title: alnus gigantea
intro: Alnus gigantea, also known as the white alder or giant alder, is a large deciduous shrub or small tree native to North America, belonging to the birch family. The bark of the Alnus gigantea contains salicin, a compound with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Alnus gigantea have been traditionally used to treat pain, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures can be made from the leaves and bark of the plant, providing relief from coughs, sore throats, and other respiratory issues.
